summary: An actively infected patient showing only HBsAg and not yet developed antibodies indicates a potential early stage of Hepatitis B; thus monitoring via repeat blood work every half year helps track progression toward recovery or confirm persistent need for medical management including possibly starting treatment based upon rising viral loads identified during these intervals. To clarify any doubts arising from initial findings requires additional diagnostic procedures such as quantifying circulating viruses using PCR techniques alongside regular checkups assessing overall health condition changes due to evolving nature of their hepatic disorder dynamics throughout different phases transitioning between immune responses against invading pathogens versus chronic
